brick: tokenize — the WHOLE text->token-ids tokenizer composed into ONE Form recipe, four-way, end-to-end from raw text
author: Sema (Opus 4.8) — sema-native-ml-walk autonomous run
claim: The three algorithmic stages of byte-level BPE are each four-way proven standalone — pre-tokenization (pretokenize.fk #3542), byte->symbol mapping (byte-to-symbol.fk #3503), and the merge core (bpe-tokenizer.fk #3447). This cell WIRES them into one callable `tokenize : text -> ids` and proves the composition on the four-kernel floor (Go/Rust/TS/fkwu) END-TO-END from RAW TEXT against the real openai/whisper-tiny tokenizer. The bpe band (#3447) proved bpe-encode on the PRE-MAPPED syms [220 83 78 74 68 77 72 89 68 81]; this proves the front-end (pretokenize -> bytes -> byte-to-symbol -> vocab-lookup) PRODUCES exactly those syms from the string ' tokenizer', and the merge core finishes -> [14862, 6545] — the whole pipeline on one floor.
north_star: A model whose weights AND tokenizer are recipe data, running on a Form-native kernel. The decode math is four-way and on the M4 GPU (multi-layer stack #3423/#3424, KV-cached decode #3412, greedy argmax #3402); the tokenizer turns text into the token ids that loop consumes. With pre-tok + byte->symbol + BPE merge each four-way AND now COMPOSED into one proven `tokenize` recipe, the tokenizer front-end is a single callable end-to-end recipe the generation loop can call — the last front-end gap before the first end-to-end native token (only the vocab + 50000-merge tables remain carrier DATA).
recipe: form/form-stdlib/tokenize.fk — tok-str-bytes (chars -> byte values, ASCII: ord c == byte), tok-vocab-id/tok-vocab-ids (codepoint -> single-symbol vocab id via the table), tok-append (flatten per-chunk id lists in order), tok-chunk-ids (one pre-token: chars->bytes->byte-to-symbol->vocab-ids->bpe-encode), tok-chunks (encode each chunk INDEPENDENTLY, concatenate), tokenize (pretokenize then tok-chunks). Composes pretokenize/bytes-to-symbols/bpe-encode verbatim — no numeric change, nothing re-derived. Pure string/int/list recursion; no host I/O, no floats — the fourth-friendly subset.
composition_law: byte-level BPE never merges across a pre-token boundary — each chunk is byte-mapped and merged INDEPENDENTLY, then per-chunk id lists are concatenated. A pair that merges WITHIN a chunk fires; the same pair STRADDLING two chunks does not. This is the load-bearing law the composition enforces and the band proves (bits 8 + 16).
reference: Python integer reference mirroring the composition exactly (re-based pretokenize oracle + the closed byte-to-symbol form + the merge core). All 5 band laws verified BEFORE authoring; the front-end of ' tokenizer' confirmed to produce [220 83 78 74 68 77 72 89 68 81] (the exact pre-mapped syms the bpe band #3447 pins), end-to-end -> [14862, 6545] the real openai/whisper-tiny output.

claims.bit_1_real_anchor_end_to_end: tokenize(' tokenizer', real-vocab-subset, real-merge-rows) -> [14862, 6545] — bit-for-bit the actual openai/whisper-tiny tokenizer, FROM THE RAW STRING (front-end produces the syms the bpe band #3447 pins, merge core finishes)
claims.bit_2_empty: tokenize('') -> [] — pretokenize gives no chunks, the flatten of nothing is nothing (the per-chunk fold base case)
claims.bit_4_cross_chunk_flatten: 'a b' -> ['a',' b'] (leading-space law); each chunk byte-maps + looks up independently, the per-chunk id lists concatenate IN ORDER -> [500,501,502] (the new behaviour the composition adds)
claims.bit_8_no_cross_boundary_merge: '1a' -> ['1','a'] (class boundary); with merge row (600,601)->700 present, the pair STRADDLES the chunk boundary so it does NOT fire -> [600,601] NOT [700] — the load-bearing byte-level-BPE law
claims.bit_16_within_chunk_merge_fires: '11' is ONE digit-run chunk; with (600,600)->800 the pair is inside the chunk so it merges -> [800]. bit 8 + bit 16 together ARE the boundary law: same pair, merges inside a chunk, refuses across chunks

proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/pretokenize.fk form-stdlib/byte-to-symbol.fk form-stdlib/bpe-tokenizer.fk form-stdlib/tokenize.fk form-stdlib/tests/tokenize-band.fk  ->  'core.fk+pretokenize.fk+byte-to-symbol.fk+bpe-tokenizer.fk+tokenize.fk+tokenize-band.fk -> 31', 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)', '1 ok, 0 divergent'. ~26s. Registered form/fourth-arm-bands.txt: 'tokenize fks 31'.
lane: COMPOSITION-by-REUSE brick (like multi-layer-stack #3423): NO new algorithm, NO numeric change — wires three already-four-way recipes into one callable, a band proving the wired pipeline reproduces the real tokenizer end-to-end. Design (the per-chunk-independent-encoding fold + the strange-minimal boundary laws bit8/bit16 + the Python reference) was the cost; the four-way proof was seconds, because the body had already built and proven all three stages.
drift_healed: RESTORED the 'pretokenize fks 255' manifest row that #3546 (sleep-classify) accidentally DELETED while adding sleep-classify/sleep-report rows. A four-way band (#3542) had been silently dropped from the fourth-arm proof floor — which CLAUDE.md forbids ('never hide it by leaving the band out of the manifest'). Restored in this PR (same tokenizer tissue) alongside the new 'tokenize fks 31' row.
next_stones: wire the FULL generation loop (tokenize prompt -> multi-layer decode stack #3423/#3424 -> greedy argmax #3402 over real llama3.2:3b GGUF) — the first end-to-end native token
the byte->symbol VOCAB table and 50000-merge table are the last carrier DATA in the tokenizer; intern them as Form data so tokenize takes only text
confirm/extend for llama3's tokenizer (tiktoken-style BPE, a different pat_str + vocab than GPT-2/whisper) — the GPU decode stack is llama, so the end-to-end token needs llama3's tokenizer
receipt_of: form/form-stdlib/tokenize.fk + form/form-stdlib/tests/tokenize-band.fk + form/fourth-arm-bands.txt (tokenize fks 31; pretokenize fks 255 restored)
